meta.label: June 2026 curriculum
meta.description: Initial public curriculum snapshot with 7 phases and ~65 resources.

learningPath.title: AI & ML Foundations
learningPath.description: Build mathematical intuition, Python fluency, and classical ML literacy before touching LLMs.

steps.title: Understand the landscape
steps.objective: Know what AI, ML, and deep learning are — and where LLMs fit.

resources.title: Machine Learning for Beginners (Microsoft)
resources.description: 26-lesson curriculum covering classical ML with Python, quizzes, and projects.

resources.title: Made With ML
resources.description: Production-focused ML course: data, training, evaluation, and MLOps patterns.

resources.title: Understanding Deep Learning
resources.description: Free textbook with visual explanations of neural networks and deep learning math.

steps.title: Design production ML systems
steps.objective: Learn how real ML systems are scoped, built, and maintained.

resources.title: Designing Machine Learning Systems
resources.description: Chip Huyen’s guide to data, modeling, deployment, and monitoring at scale.

resources.title: Awesome Generative AI Guide
resources.description: Curated hub: papers, interview prep, free courses, and applied LLM notebooks.

steps.title: Stay current from day one
steps.objective: Subscribe to newsletters that filter signal from the AI noise.

resources.title: Gradient Ascent
resources.description: Weekly AI digest trusted by researchers and engineers — papers, demos, and essays.

resources.title: Data Hustle
resources.description: Career and practical data skills — useful while building your AI foundation.

learningPath.title: LLM Fundamentals
learningPath.description: Understand how transformers work, how to prompt effectively, and how LLMs are built.

steps.title: What are LLMs?
steps.objective: Build intuition for language models before writing agent code.

resources.title: LLM Introduction
resources.description: Accessible overview of what LLMs are and why they matter for builders.

resources.title: Hands-On Large Language Models
resources.description: “The Illustrated LLM Book” — 300+ figures, Colab notebooks, RAG and fine-tuning.

resources.title: LLM Course (mlabonne)
resources.description: Structured roadmap: LLM fundamentals, fine-tuning, quantization, and deployment.

resources.title: How Transformer LLMs Work
resources.description: Jay Alammar & Maarten Grootendorst explain the architecture behind modern LLMs.

steps.title: Prompt engineering mastery
steps.objective: Learn to communicate reliably with LLMs — the skill every agent builds on.

resources.title: Prompt Engineering Guide
resources.description: Comprehensive guide: techniques, RAG, agents, model-specific tips, and papers.

resources.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
resources.description: Seminal paper showing how step-by-step reasoning dramatically improves LLM accuracy.

steps.title: Build an LLM from scratch
steps.objective: Demystify the black box by implementing core components yourself.

resources.title: LLMs from Scratch
resources.description: Video walkthrough of building and training a language model from first principles.

resources.title: Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)
resources.description: Sebastian Raschka’s hands-on book + code: GPT architecture, pretraining, fine-tuning.

learningPath.title: Applied LLM Engineering
learningPath.description: RAG, embeddings, vector search, and evaluation — the skills behind production LLM apps.

steps.title: Retrieval & vector databases
steps.objective: Store and retrieve knowledge so LLMs can answer beyond their training data.

resources.title: Building Applications with Vector Databases
resources.description: Six Pinecone apps: semantic search, RAG, recommenders, hybrid search, anomaly detection.

resources.title: Vector Databases: from Embeddings to Applications
resources.description: Design real-world vector search apps with Weaviate — hybrid and multilingual search.

resources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation Survey
resources.description: Comprehensive survey of RAG architectures, evaluation, and open challenges.

steps.title: Build and evaluate RAG apps
steps.objective: Go from naive retrieval to production-grade RAG with proper evaluation.

resources.title: Building and Evaluating Advanced RAG
resources.description: Sentence-window, auto-merging retrieval, and evaluation with LlamaIndex + TruEra.

resources.title: Improving Accuracy of LLM Applications
resources.description: Systematic accuracy improvements via evaluation, prompting, and memory tuning.

resources.title: The LLM Engineering Handbook
resources.description: End-to-end LLM Twin project: data pipelines, fine-tuning, RAG, AWS deployment, LLMOps.

resources.title: Hands-On AI Engineering
resources.description: Practical AI engineering patterns, projects, and production workflows.

steps.title: Deep technical reading
steps.objective: Follow researchers who explain LLM internals and evaluation rigorously.

resources.title: Deep (Learning) Focus
resources.description: Netflix research scientist explains transformers, SFT, RL, and agent evaluation in depth.

resources.title: DecodingML
resources.description: Paul Iusztin on designing, building, and shipping AI software from idea to production.

learningPath.title: Agent Foundations
learningPath.description: Understand agent architectures, core papers, and design patterns before building your own.

steps.title: What is agentic AI?
steps.objective: Learn the theory and vocabulary behind autonomous LLM systems.

resources.title: Agentic AI Overview (Stanford)
resources.description: Academic overview of agentic AI concepts, capabilities, and research directions.

resources.title: Google's Agent Whitepaper
resources.description: Models, tools, and orchestration — cognitive architectures for generative AI agents.

resources.title: Agentic AI (Andrew Ng)
resources.description: Build agentic systems with reflection, tool use, planning, and multi-agent patterns.

steps.title: Foundational agent papers
steps.objective: Read the research that defines modern agent behavior.

resources.title: ReAct: Reasoning + Acting
resources.description: Interleave reasoning traces with tool actions — the pattern behind most agents today.

resources.title: Toolformer
resources.description: Self-supervised tool learning — models teach themselves when and how to call APIs.

resources.title: Tree of Thoughts
resources.description: Deliberate problem-solving via branching reasoning trees and backtracking.

steps.title: Agent design best practices
steps.objective: Learn from Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google how to build agents that actually work.

resources.title: Building Effective Agents (Anthropic)
resources.description: Start simple, use composable patterns, craft tool interfaces — Anthropic’s playbook.

resources.title: OpenAI's Practical Guide to Building Agents
resources.description: Use-case identification, orchestration patterns, guardrails, and human-in-the-loop.

resources.title: Building Effective Agents
resources.description: Talk expanding on Anthropic’s agent design principles with real-world examples.

resources.title: Google's Agent Companion
resources.description: Advanced topics: agentic RAG, evaluation frameworks, multi-agent systems, Agent Ops.

steps.title: Hands-on agent courses & repos
steps.objective: Start building with guided curricula and open-source examples.

resources.title: AI Agents for Beginners (Microsoft)
resources.description: 12-lesson course: agent fundamentals, frameworks, RAG, planning, multi-agent, MCP.

resources.title: HuggingFace's Agent Course
resources.description: Free certified course: smolagents, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, and community leaderboard.

resources.title: GenAI Agents
resources.description: All-in-one repo: tutorials and implementations for diverse GenAI agent use cases.

resources.title: AI Agentic Design Patterns with AutoGen
resources.description: Microsoft + Penn State: multi-agent systems with diverse roles using AutoGen.

learningPath.title: Building Production Agents
learningPath.description: MCP, memory, evaluation, browser agents, and shipping agents users can trust.

steps.title: Build agents with MCP
steps.objective: Connect agents to tools and data using the industry-standard protocol.

resources.title: Building Agents with MCP
resources.description: Video guide to Model Context Protocol for tool-augmented agent systems.

resources.title: MCP with Anthropic
resources.description: Build MCP servers with FastMCP, connect Claude Desktop, deploy remotely.

resources.title: AI Agents with MCP
resources.description: Kyle Stratis: MCP servers, clients, transport layers, and agent workflows in Python.

steps.title: Agent memory & state
steps.objective: Build agents that persist knowledge and improve across sessions.

resources.title: Agent Memory: Building Memory-Aware Agents
resources.description: Memory-first architecture: persistent stores, semantic tool retrieval, write-back loops.

resources.title: Generative Agents
resources.description: Simulated human-like agents with memory, reflection, and planning (Stanford/Google).

steps.title: Build and evaluate agents
steps.objective: Implement agents from scratch and measure them systematically.

resources.title: Building and Evaluating Agents
resources.description: End-to-end walkthrough of agent construction and structured evaluation.

resources.title: Building an Agent from Scratch
resources.description: Minimal agent implementation — understand every component before using frameworks.

resources.title: Evaluating AI Agents
resources.description: Code-based and LLM-as-judge evaluators, router/skill testing, trace-based experiments.

resources.title: Reflexion
resources.description: Verbal reinforcement learning — agents reflect on failures and improve iteratively.

steps.title: Specialized agent capabilities
steps.objective: Browser automation, computer use, and agentic coding workflows.

resources.title: Building Browser Agents
resources.description: Agents that navigate and interact with websites — reliability patterns included.

resources.title: Computer Use with Anthropic
resources.description: How AI assistants perceive and act on desktop environments.

resources.title: Claude Code Best Agentic Coding Practices
resources.description: Context engineering, CLAUDE.md, skills, verification loops, and permission modes.

steps.title: Production AI engineering books
steps.objective: Deepen with practitioner-authored guides for shipping agent systems.

resources.title: AI Agents: The Definitive Guide
resources.description: Nicole Koenigstein: production agent design, tool integration, safety, and scaling.

resources.title: Building Applications with AI Agents
resources.description: Michael Albada: single- and multi-agent design patterns from concept to deployment.

resources.title: AI Engineering
resources.description: Chip Huyen on foundation models, use cases, adaptation, evaluation, and deployment.

resources.title: Jam with AI
resources.description: Production-grade AI/ML systems — RAG pipelines, agents, and MLOps from practitioners.

learningPath.title: Expert Mastery
learningPath.description: Multi-agent orchestration, LLMOps at scale, research depth, and system-level thinking.

steps.title: Multi-agent systems
steps.objective: Orchestrate teams of specialized agents for complex workflows.

resources.title: Multi-Agent Use (crewAI Advanced)
resources.description: Parallel, sequential, and hybrid multi-agent setups with performance optimization.

resources.title: Design Multi-Agent Systems with CrewAI
resources.description: Production deployment: flows, A2A protocol, observability, CI/CD for agents.

resources.title: Multi Agent Systems (DeepLearning.AI)
resources.description: Automate business workflows with multi-agent teams exceeding single-LLM performance.

resources.title: Philo Agents (Playlist)
resources.description: Deep philosophical and technical exploration of agent architectures and cognition.

steps.title: LLMOps & production reliability
steps.objective: Operate LLM systems with CI, monitoring, and automated testing.

resources.title: LLMOps
resources.description: Fine-tune and deploy LLMs on Google Cloud with automated MLOps pipelines.

resources.title: Automated Testing for LLMOps
resources.description: CI workflows with rules-based and model-graded evals using CircleCI.

steps.title: Research frontier & community
steps.objective: Stay at the cutting edge through papers, playlists, and expert voices.

resources.title: NeoSage
resources.description: Shivani’s blog on advanced AI research, agents, and emerging paradigms.

learningPath.title: AI News Radar
learningPath.description: Stay current with the AI landscape and turn every headline into a deliberate learning action — powered by GetStream's awesome-ai-news.

steps.title: Your monthly news hub
steps.objective: Establish a repeatable ritual to scan AI news without drowning in hype.

resources.title: Awesome AI News (GetStream)
resources.description: Monthly curated AI news for developers — models, agents, voice, vision, tools. Check the README each month.

resources.title: Software Is Changing (Again) — Andrej Karpathy
resources.description: Software 3.0 keynote: LLMs as a new computing paradigm, build-for-agents, and the decade of partial autonomy.

steps.title: Agent infrastructure standards
steps.objective: Understand the emerging standards your team and tools will converge on.

resources.title: AGENTS.md Standard
resources.description: Cross-tool standard for AI agent instructions — read by Codex, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, and more.

resources.title: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK)
resources.description: Google's open-source toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying agents — pairs with Gemini.

resources.title: OpenAI Agents Guide & Builder
resources.description: OpenAI's agent documentation — Agent Builder, SDK, guardrails, and orchestration patterns.

steps.title: Emerging modalities from the news
steps.objective: Voice and vision agents are dominating headlines — know where to go deep.

resources.title: Building AI Voice Agents for Production
resources.description: Responsive, scalable voice AI — directly relevant to gpt-realtime, Grok Voice, and Gemini Live news.

resources.title: Vision Agents (GetStream)
resources.description: Open-source framework for real-time voice + video AI agents — from the same team as awesome-ai-news.

full.label: Full Track
full.subtitle: Complete beginner → expert
full.summary: Every resource in sequence — for hands-on builders starting from scratch.

swe-manager.label: Software Engineering Manager
swe-manager.subtitle: Lead AI teams with confidence
swe-manager.summary: You already understand systems, shipping, and people. This track skips deep math and from-scratch implementation — and focuses on architecture, evaluation, production trade-offs, and the vocabulary to lead AI engineering teams.
goals: Evaluate agent/RAG proposals and vendor pitches critically
Ask the right questions in design reviews and sprint planning
Understand when to use workflows vs agents, RAG vs fine-tuning
Set up evaluation and LLMOps expectations for your team
Stay current without reading every paper or writing training code

foundations.description: Lite track only — you don't need a full ML course. Focus on system design literacy and curated references.
foundations.note: Skim DMLS + subscribe to a newsletter. Skip UDL and full ML bootcamps.

llm-fundamentals.description: Build LLM fluency fast — enough to read architecture docs and lead technical discussions.
llm-fundamentals.note: Watch intro videos, read the prompt guide, take the 1-hr transformers course.

applied-llm.description: RAG and vector search — what your team will actually ship. Prioritize evaluation over implementation.
applied-llm.note: Understand RAG trade-offs; let your team handle Pinecone/Weaviate setup details.

agent-foundations.description: Your highest-ROI phase — industry guides, agent architecture, and design patterns for leadership.
agent-foundations.note: Read Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google whitepapers before any framework course.

production-agents.description: MCP, memory, evaluation, and production books — how to scope and govern agent projects.
production-agents.note: Evaluating AI Agents course is non-negotiable for managers.

expert-mastery.description: Multi-agent strategy and LLMOps — for scaling teams and long-horizon planning.
expert-mastery.note: Pick multi-agent overview + LLMOps; skip philosophical deep dives unless interested.

ai-news-radar.description: Your ongoing news ritual — scan awesome-ai-news monthly and map headlines to curriculum.
ai-news-radar.note: Start this in week 1 alongside LLM fundamentals. Check GitHub on the 1st Monday each month.

resourceNotes.dmls: Best ROI for managers — mirrors how you already think about production systems.
resourceNotes.llm-intro-video: Watch first. Gives you vocabulary for every conversation after.
resourceNotes.hands-on-llm: Skim Part 1 + RAG chapters. Skip fine-tuning unless your team does it.
resourceNotes.prompt-guide: Read the techniques section — you'll use this in every AI product review.
resourceNotes.improve-accuracy: Teaches the evaluation mindset your team needs before shipping.
resourceNotes.stanford-agents: Academic framing helps you push back on over-engineered agent proposals.
resourceNotes.anthropic-agents: Must-read. "Don't build agents when workflows will do" — quote this in meetings.
resourceNotes.openai-agents: Guardrails and orchestration patterns — use for scoping docs.
resourceNotes.eval-agents: Critical. You can't manage what you can't measure.
resourceNotes.ai-agents-definitive: Production agent pitfalls — agent-washing, cost, reliability.
resourceNotes.ai-engineering: Chip Huyen's strategic view on foundation models in products.
resourceNotes.llmops: Set expectations for CI, deployment, and monitoring on AI projects.
resourceNotes.awesome-ai-news: Bookmark this repo. Your 15-min monthly scan replaces Twitter doom-scrolling.
resourceNotes.karpathy-software-changing: Software 3.0 framing — use in strategy docs and team talks.
resourceNotes.agents-md: If your team uses coding agents, require AGENTS.md in every repo.

product-manager.label: Product Manager
product-manager.subtitle: Ship AI products with clarity
product-manager.summary: Lead AI product discovery and delivery without writing training code. Prioritizes user value, evaluation, guardrails, and cross-functional vocabulary — similar to the manager track with product-centric framing.
goals: Write crisp PRDs for LLM and agent features
Choose between RAG, fine-tuning, and tool use for product bets
Define success metrics and eval criteria before launch
Communicate trade-offs to engineering and leadership

llm-fundamentals.description: Product vocabulary for LLMs — prompts, context windows, and failure modes.
llm-fundamentals.note: Focus on prompt guide + intro videos; skip implementation-heavy repos.

applied-llm.description: RAG and retrieval for product specs — when search beats fine-tuning.
applied-llm.note: Understand accuracy improvement patterns; partner with eng on vector DB choice.

agent-foundations.description: Agent UX patterns — when autonomy helps vs hurts the user journey.
agent-foundations.note: Read lab playbooks; prioritize eval and guardrails over framework depth.

production-agents.description: Launch checklists — eval, cost, reliability, and MCP integrations.
production-agents.note: Evaluating AI Agents course is essential for PMs owning AI launches.

ai-news-radar.description: Monthly signal for roadmap bets and stakeholder updates.
ai-news-radar.note: Map headlines to PRD themes and learning bridges.

resourceNotes.prompt-guide: Your spec-writing superpower for LLM features.
resourceNotes.improve-accuracy: Frame eval loops in product terms before beta.
resourceNotes.anthropic-agents: Quote workflow-vs-agent guidance in roadmap reviews.
resourceNotes.eval-agents: Define launch gates and regression suites with your team.
resourceNotes.awesome-ai-news: Monthly scan for roadmap and exec briefings.

ic-engineer.label: IC Engineer
ic-engineer.subtitle: Hands-on builder track
ic-engineer.summary: Full technical depth — implement RAG, agents, and production patterns yourself. Same comprehensive resource set as the full track, sequenced for individual contributors shipping AI features.
goals: Build and debug RAG pipelines end-to-end
Implement agent workflows with evaluation from day one
Ship production-ready LLM features with proper observability
Stay current with frameworks without chasing every release

data-scientist.label: Data Scientist
data-scientist.subtitle: ML foundations → applied LLMs
data-scientist.summary: Bridge classical ML literacy with modern LLM/RAG engineering. Emphasizes foundations, embeddings, retrieval, and evaluation — lighter on multi-agent production ops unless you need them.
goals: Connect traditional ML intuition to transformer-based systems
Design RAG and embedding pipelines with measurable quality
Evaluate LLM outputs with rigorous experiment design
Know when to fine-tune vs retrieve vs prompt

foundations.description: Core ML literacy — don't skip this phase.
foundations.note: DMLS + hands-on ML projects before deep LLM work.

applied-llm.description: Your home turf — RAG, vectors, and accuracy at scale.
applied-llm.note: Prioritize RAG surveys, vector DBs, and accuracy guides.

agent-foundations.description: Agent patterns for data-heavy workflows — lighter than IC eng depth.
agent-foundations.note: Focus on overview guides; skip deep framework courses unless needed.

production-agents.description: Eval and LLMOps — optional unless you own deployment.

resourceNotes.dmls: Anchor your ML → LLM mental model here.
resourceNotes.hands-on-llm: Work through RAG and fine-tuning chapters hands-on.
resourceNotes.rag-survey: Canonical reference for retrieval architecture choices.
resourceNotes.improve-accuracy: Experiment design for LLM quality — your core skill.
resourceNotes.eval-agents: Apply eval rigor even if you are not shipping agents yet.
resourceNotes.llmops: Monitoring and regression for models in production.

awesomeAiNews.title: Awesome AI News (GetStream)
awesomeAiNews.description: Monthly curated AI news for developers — model releases, agent tools, voice/vision SDKs, and frameworks. Your primary radar for what changed this month.
awesomeAiNews.updateCadence: Check the 1st Monday of each month (~15 min)

monthlyRitual.title: Scan the latest month
monthlyRitual.detail: Open awesome-ai-news and read the current month section top-to-bottom.

monthlyRitual.title: Tag each headline
monthlyRitual.detail: Match headlines to a theme below (agents, voice, coding, models, RAG, multi-agent). Ignore pure product launches unless your team uses them.

monthlyRitual.title: Pick one learning action
monthlyRitual.detail: Choose 1 headline → 1 curriculum resource to study or assign to your team that week.

monthlyRitual.title: Discuss in staff meeting
monthlyRitual.detail: Share one insight: "This news means we should / shouldn't …" using the manager action prompts.

learningBridges.newsSignal: New agent frameworks, Agent SDKs, "agentic IDE" launches, Eve/Flue/Open Agents
recentExamples: Eve by Vercel — agent framework like Next.js for agents
learningBridges.managerAction: Ask: Is this a framework our team should standardize on, or noise? Compare against Anthropic's "start with workflows" principle.

learningBridges.newsSignal: Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot CLI, agentic coding in Xcode/VS Code
recentExamples: Codex OSS Mode — use any open-source model
learningBridges.managerAction: Define team standards: AGENTS.md, eval harness, and when agents get write access. Measure productivity, not just adoption.

learningBridges.newsSignal: MCP servers, Agent Skills, A2A protocol, openresponses.org, UCP commerce
learningBridges.managerAction: Treat MCP as your team's USB-C for tools — standardize tool interfaces before multiplying agents.

learningBridges.newsSignal: gpt-realtime, Grok Voice, Deepgram Flux, ElevenLabs agents, Gemini Live
recentExamples: Deepgram Flux Multilingual for voice agents
MoshiRAG — async retrieval for speech models
learningBridges.managerAction: Voice agents need latency budgets + eval metrics different from text. Pilot one use case before platform bet.

learningBridges.newsSignal: GPT-5.x, Claude Opus/Sonnet, Gemini 3.x, Qwen, DeepSeek, open-weight models
recentExamples: Ollama + local model support for Codex/Claude Code
learningBridges.managerAction: Don't chase every release. Ask: does this change our cost/latency/quality tradeoff for a specific use case?

learningBridges.newsSignal: Built-in RAG, agentic RAG, vector search, file search in APIs
recentExamples: Gemini Built-In RAG: File Search in the API
MoshiRAG — async knowledge for speech LLMs
Vision Agents RAG + File Search plugins
Google Code Wiki — auto architecture diagrams
learningBridges.managerAction: News often announces features — your team still needs eval pipelines. Require retrieval benchmarks before production.

learningBridges.newsSignal: Agent swarms, multi-agent crews, RalphTUI loops, managed cloud agents
recentExamples: Managed Antigravity Agents in Gemini API
Design Multi-Agent Systems with CrewAI course
learningBridges.managerAction: Multi-agent adds coordination cost. Require proof that single-agent + workflow failed first.

learningBridges.newsSignal: OpenAI Frontier, enterprise platforms, observability, automated testing
recentExamples: VS Code OpenTelemetry tracing for agents
learningBridges.managerAction: Platform news ≠ production readiness. Insist on CI evals, guardrails, and cost monitoring before enterprise rollout.

recentHighlights.headline: Eve by Vercel — agent framework like Next.js for agents
recentHighlights.learningAction: Read Anthropic Building Effective Agents — compare framework vs workflow

recentHighlights.headline: Codex OSS Mode — use any open-source model with Codex
recentHighlights.learningAction: Review Claude Code best practices + eval-agents course

recentHighlights.headline: Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
recentHighlights.learningAction: Explore voice agents course + Vision Agents framework

recentHighlights.headline: Managed Antigravity Agents in Gemini API
recentHighlights.learningAction: Study Google Agent Companion whitepaper on multi-agent eval

recentHighlights.headline: Gemini Built-In RAG: File Search in the API
recentHighlights.learningAction: Complete Building and Evaluating Advanced RAG course

recentHighlights.headline: OpenAI Agent Builder & Agent SDK
recentHighlights.learningAction: Read OpenAI Practical Guide + try Agent Builder docs
